reasoning: The document suffers from severe content loss and structural failure, specifically the omission of core tabular data on pages 6 and 8 and the replacement of all maps and charts with monochromatic placeholder blocks. While programmatic text recall is high (93.7%), the loss of the presentation's primary data visualizations and the corruption of table structures make the document functionally unusable without significant manual intervention.

concerns.analysis: The output fails to extract the tabular data present on page 6 (Ground Truth Locations) and page 8 (Elevation Difference data table). Specifically, page 6 contains a table with 23 data rows that is entirely absent from the structured JSON output. Similarly, page 8 contains a data table with approximately 20 rows that is not converted to a table element.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 6 contains a table with headers (Longitude, Latitude, Location, State, etc.) and 23 rows of data. Page 8 contains a data table at the bottom left with approximately 20 rows of elevation/volume data.
concerns.output_quote: The element lists for Section 6 (page 6) and Section 8 (page 8) contain no 'table' type elements, and the data is not present in paragraph format. The output contains 0 rows for these tables.
concerns.location: Sections 6 and 8, corresponding to pages 6 and 8.

concerns.description: Significant text omission within a section
concerns.analysis: In Section 6 (page 6) and Section 8 (page 8), the omission of the primary data tables constitutes a loss of more than 25% of the information content for those sections. On page 6, the table of coordinates and locations is the core of the slide but is missing from the output text/structure.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Slide 6 contains a large table of ground-truth locations (23 rows). Slide 8 contains a detailed data table supporting the map and chart visualization.
concerns.output_quote: The textual and structured representation of these sections in the JSON omits the data rows entirely, including only headings, slogans, and images.

concerns.analysis: The output contains duplicate text blocks within Section 8. The recurring header 'BUILDING STRONG Trusted Partners Delivering Value, Today and Tomorrow' is used as the title for Section 8 and is then repeated as a 'heading' element immediately within the same section. In the source PDF (Page 8), this text appears only once as a header. This violates the rule against text appearing more times in the output than in the source within a single context.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: BUILDING STRONG Trusted Partners Delivering Value, Today and Tomorrow (Header on Page 8)
concerns.output_quote: The text appears both as 'title': 'BUILDING STRONG Trusted Partners Delivering Value, Today and Tomorrow' and as a 'heading' element with the same text in Section 8.

concerns.analysis: The heading hierarchy is inconsistent. While Section 5 correctly uses H2 for the section title and H3 for a nested heading, Section 11 and Section 8 use H2 for both the section title and the nested heading elements. In Section 11, slide titles from pages 12, 13, and 14 are nested under the 'Hurricane Matthew' section (Page 11) but are assigned level 2, the same level as the parent title. This results in a flat hierarchy for nested content, which is a structural defect.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Titles on Pages 12, 13, and 14 nested under Page 11 context.
concerns.output_quote: Section 11 has 'title': 'Hurricane Matthew' (H2) and nested headings with 'level': 2.

concerns.analysis: The source document contains a clear, text-based table on Page 6 titled 'Post-Matthew Ground Truth Locations' with five distinct columns (Longitude, Latitude, Location, State, Cell Phones). The conversion output fails to identify or preserve this structure as a 'table' type. Instead, it is grouped into an image element and a paragraph, losing all structural semantic information and row/column data organization.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Table with columns 'Longitude', 'Latitude', 'Location', 'State', 'Cell Phones' on Page 6.
concerns.output_quote: The table is entirely missing from the JSON structure, replaced by an image description: 'Map of the US East Coast and a table of ground truth locations acquired by USGS.'

concerns.analysis: The HTML conversion significantly changes the visual organization of the source document. The original PDF is a slide presentation where each page integrates text with complex visual data (maps, charts, background graphics). The HTML output collapses this into a single-column flow and replaces the primary visual content with large monochromatic grey and green blocks. This destroys the visual intent and makes it difficult for a user to find information in a manner consistent with the source docum
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The source PDF (pages 4-14) uses a presentation slide layout where text overlays or is positioned alongside maps and complex infographics.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML screenshot shows a continuous vertical list of headers followed by massive solid-colored boxes (e.g., the large green block under the page 6 header 'Post-Matthew Ground Truth Locations').

concerns.analysis: The large table of 'Post-Matthew Ground Truth Locations' found on page 6 of the PDF is completely missing from the HTML output. It is not rendered as text, a table structure, or even an image; it is simply replaced by a large green placeholder block along with the map content.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: In the source PDF, page 6 shows a 23-row table containing columns for Longitude, Latitude, Location, and State.
concerns.output_quote: In the HTML screenshot, the section 'Post-Matthew Ground Truth Locations' contains only a large green block and some surrounding text; the table data is absent.

concerns.analysis: The conversion fails to render almost all critical, non-decorative images from the source document. Maps, data visualizations, and charts that constitute the core information of the presentation (on pages 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 11, 12, 13, and 14) are missing and replaced by solid color blocks (grey or green). Only the USACE logo on page 4 is visible.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Meaningful maps and charts on pages 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 11, 12, 13, and 14 (e.g., the operations overview map on page 5).
concerns.output_quote: The HTML screenshot shows solid colored boxes where these images should be (e.g., a grey box for page 5's map and green boxes for content on subsequent pages).

concerns.analysis: The visual quality of the converted document is extremely poor. The failure to render the primary visual elements (maps and tables) leaves the document filled with large, empty-looking monochromatic blocks. This creates an unprofessional appearance and makes the document functionally useless for its intended purpose of sharing geographic data.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The source PDF is a polished professional presentation with high-resolution maps and integrated graphics.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML screenshot shows a series of text headers followed by large, unattractive green and grey placeholder boxes.
concerns.location: Entire document, notably from page 4 onwards

action_items: Manually extract and format the 'Post-Matthew Ground Truth Locations' table from page 6
Restore the elevation difference data table on page 8
Re-integrate critical map and chart images that were replaced by monochromatic blocks
Correct the heading levels in the 'Hurricane Matthew' section to ensure slide titles are properly nested
